mechanism_narrative: A lower FEDFUNDS rate reduces the discount rate on equity cash flows and supports higher S&P 500 prices over the next month. The signal is positive when policy is not tightening and short rates are drifting down or flat.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are negative in a majority of periods with falling or flat FEDFUNDS, or if rolling 3-year rank correlation stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals weakening household income and demand, which tends to precede negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is strongest when unemployment is accelerating rather than stable.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances produce positive 20-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the sign of the effect is not negative in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and HOUST both weaken, it signals broad consumer demand and housing demand contraction, which should weigh on equities over the next quarter. The joint deterioration matters because one weak series alone is less informative than synchronized demand softening.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to correspond to negative 60-day SPX returns with at least 60% accuracy, or if either series alone fully explains the effect.

mechanism_narrative: A rising INDPRO path indicates improving industrial output and operating leverage for cyclicals, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is strongest when production is above its recent trend and not offset by labor deterioration.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises over the latest 2 monthly observations
failure_condition: Reject if rising INDPRO is followed by negative 20-day SPX returns more often than positive returns, or if the effect disappears after one month of sample expansion.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized rise in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates expanding payroll income and retail spending, which should support earnings expectations and push the S&P 500 higher over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism fails when employment rises without sales confirmation.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS and RSAFS both rise versus prior month
failure_condition: Reject if joint PAYEMS/RSAFS improvements do not outperform random chance for 20-day SPX direction, or if the positive effect remains when sales are falling.

mechanism_narrative: A high FEDFUNDS level above neutral compresses valuation multiples and tightens financial conditions, creating downward pressure on S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is negative because discounting dominates when policy is restrictive.
failure_condition: Reject if SPX 20-day returns are not negative in a majority of periods with FEDFUNDS above 4.5%, or if the signal is weaker than baseline after controlling for recent inflation.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in PAYEMS relative to trend signals labor-market deterioration and slower future consumption, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next week. The signal is a short-horizon warning because payroll weakness is quickly priced into growth expectations.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines on the latest monthly print
failure_condition: Reject if payroll declines are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the effect disappears outside recession scare periods.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth is weak and housing starts are also falling, the economy is likely entering a demand slowdown that hurts earnings estimates and equity prices over the next quarter. The mechanism is specific to simultaneous weakness in output and housing activity.
failure_condition: Reject if weak GDPC1 growth combined with falling HOUST does not predict negative 60-day SPX returns with at least 60% directional accuracy, or if the effect is positive during deceleration episodes.
